label: Heroku
description: Connect to Heroku to manage apps, dynos, pipelines, and deployments on the Heroku PaaS.

form.title: Connect Heroku
form.subtitle: Paste your Heroku API key to let Anton manage your apps and infrastructure.

methods.label: API Key
methods.description: Paste your personal Heroku API key from Account Settings. No OAuth setup required — the simplest and fastest way to connect.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with a Heroku API Key

Heroku issues a personal API key tied to your account that grants full API access.

### Steps

1. Log in to [Heroku Dashboard](https://dashboard.heroku.com).
2. Click your avatar or account menu in the top-right corner and select **Account settings**.
3. Scroll down to the **API Key** section.
4. Click **Reveal** to display your key, then copy it.
5. Paste the key into the **API Key** field below.

### Notes

- The API key grants full access to your Heroku account — treat it like a password.
- If you need to revoke access, return to **Account settings → API Key** and click **Regenerate API Key**. Any previous key is immediately invalidated.
- For team or enterprise setups, consider creating a dedicated Heroku account with limited access and using its API key instead.
- Read [Heroku's API authentication docs](https://devcenter.heroku.com/articles/platform-api-reference#authentication) for full details.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: Found in Heroku Dashboard → Account settings → API Key → Reveal.

fields.name: account_email
fields.label: Account Email
fields.description: Optional. Your Heroku account email, used to confirm which account is connected.

methods.label: OAuth (Bring Your Own Client)
methods.description: Use a Heroku OAuth client for delegated access. Requires creating an OAuth client in your Heroku account first — recommended for multi-user or production integrations.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with Heroku OAuth

Heroku supports OAuth 2.0 for delegated access without sharing your personal API key.

### Steps

1. Install the [Heroku CLI](https://devcenter.heroku.com/articles/heroku-cli) if you haven't already.
2. Run `heroku clients:create Anton http://localhost` to register a new OAuth client. Heroku returns a **Client ID** and **Client Secret**.
3. Copy those values into the fields below.
4. Choose a scope — `global` gives full access; use a narrower scope if your Heroku plan supports it.
5. Click **Submit** to open a browser window where you authorize the OAuth client.

### Notes

- OAuth clients are managed via `heroku clients` CLI commands or the Platform API.
- The `global` scope mirrors full API key access. Check [Heroku OAuth scopes](https://devcenter.heroku.com/articles/oauth#scopes) for narrower options.
- Tokens can be revoked from **Account settings → Authorized applications** or via the API.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: The client ID returned when you ran `heroku clients:create`.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: The client secret returned alongside the client ID. Keep this private.
